questionText: A projectile has position $x = 24t$, $y = 10t - 5t^2$ (metres) at time $t$ seconds. Find the times when the projectile is at height $y = 5$ m.

steps.explanation: We want the times at which the vertical position is $5$ m.
Bringing all terms to one side gives a quadratic in $t$.
This is a perfect square, giving a single repeated root.

steps.workingLatex: t = 1 \text{ s (turning point at this height)}
steps.explanation: The single solution means $y = 5$ is the maximum height, reached exactly once, at $t = 1$ s.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: $t = 1$ s (this height is the peak, reached once).
